body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Nonordinary Rendering** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Psychedelics — DMN disruption, ego dissolution, altered states, predictive processing decoupling.

**Alterations in brain network connectivity and subjective experience induced by psychedelics: ascoping review.**. Front Psychiatry.. PMID: 38807690. DOI: 10.3389/fpsyt.2024.1386321. Intense interest surrounds current research on psychedelics, particularly regarding their potential in treating mental health disorders. Various studies suggest alink between the subjective effects produced by psychedelics and their therapeutic efficacy. Neuroimaging evidence indicates an association of changes in brain functional connectivity with the subjective effects of psychedelics.
